classification.reasoning: The patch introduces new logic to handle out-of-memory (OOM) scenarios by broadcasting kill requests to other nodes when failures occur during distributed result streaming. It adds new imports, fields, constructor parameters, and methods, and modifies existing methods to integrate this kill broadcast mechanism. The changes are mostly additive and involve some control flow adjustments but do not restructure the code significantly or change core APIs. The localization evidence shows a mix of exact and pickaxe matches with high confidence, indicating the patch applies with some fuzzy matching due to added logic and parameters. Overall, the patch complexity fits TYPE_II as it requires fuzzy matching and moderate understanding to backport correctly, but no deep structural refactoring or redesign is involved.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a static import for the broadcastKill method from TransportDistributedResultAction.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable direct usage of broadcastKill method without qualifying its class, facilitating job kill broadcasts.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Imported classes related to job killing: KillJobsNodeRequest and KillResponse.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support sending kill requests and handling kill responses for distributed jobs.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added two new fields: killNodeAction (an ActionExecutor for kill requests) and localNodeId (string).
hunk_descriptions.intent: To hold references needed to send kill requests and identify the local node for job cancellation purposes.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Extended constructor parameters to include killNodeAction and localNodeId.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To allow injection of the kill action executor and local node identifier when creating DistributingConsumer instances.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Assigned the new killNodeAction and localNodeId fields inside the constructor.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To initialize the new fields for use in job kill operations and local node identification.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Replaced logic on non-failure requests to broadcast a kill signal to downstream nodes, close iterator, and complete future exceptionally on failure.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: DistributingConsumer, inside response handling logic (likely in a callback or method handling downstream responses)
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure that when an error occurs, all downstream nodes are informed to kill the job, preventing stuck or inconsistent states.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added imports for KillJobsNodeAction, KillJobsNodeRequest, and KillResponse.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable the factory to create DistributingConsumer instances that can send kill requests.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new field killNodeAction of type ActionExecutor for kill requests.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To hold the executor responsible for sending kill job requests to nodes.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Initialized killNodeAction in the constructor to execute KillJobsNodeAction on the node's client.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To prepare the killNodeAction executor for use when creating DistributingConsumer instances.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Modified the create method call to pass killNodeAction and local node ID as additional parameters to DistributingConsumer constructor.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To provide the necessary kill action executor and local node identifier to the DistributingConsumer instances it creates.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of java.util.UUID.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support usage of UUID type in this class, likely for job or request identification.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Changed the boolean parameter controlling request handling from false to true in the superclass constructor call.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure that all requests are handled to prevent jobs from getting stuck, even if they are failures.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Replaced inline kill request logic on missing RootTask with a call to broadcastKill method; added broadcastKill static method to send kill requests to all nodes except the sender; improved logging and error handling.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: TransportDistributedResultAction.handleRequest method (failure handling path) and static utility methods
hunk_descriptions.intent: To centralize and standardize the logic for broadcasting kill requests when job context is missing, preventing handler deadlocks and stuck jobs.
